featureRequest: Add OAuth 2.0 authentication to support single sign-on (SSO) across multiple applications in the platform

stage1_requirementBrief.featureRequest: Add OAuth 2.0 authentication to support single sign-on (SSO) across multiple applications in the platform
objectives: Enable seamless SSO experience across platform
Maintain backward compatibility with existing API keys
constraints: Must comply with GDPR and CCPA data privacy regulations
Must support both web and mobile clients
successCriteria: Users can authenticate via OAuth 2.0 from any client
Zero breaking changes to existing API
assumptions: Third-party identity provider (e.g., Auth0) will be used
Backend infrastructure can handle increased load

risks.description: Token compromise could grant unauthorized access
Increased authentication latency under high load
Legacy clients may not support OAuth

stage2_architectureReview.summary: OAuth 2.0 architecture is sound and aligns with industry standards. Recommends token caching layer and separate identity service. Security review passed with conditions on token refresh mechanism.

layerReview.assessment: Login UI/flows must support redirect-based OAuth. Consider using OAuth 2.0 PKCE for mobile clients.
concerns: Mobile client token handling not detailed

layerReview.assessment: Token generation, validation, and refresh logic properly separated. Recommend caching layer for token validation.
Token storage requires encryption at rest. Consider token versioning for rotation.

dependencies.reason: Third-party OAuth provider for user authentication

scalability.concern: Token validation must support 1000+ concurrent sessions
scalability.recommendation: Implement distributed token cache with Redis

security.description: Tokens stored in browser localStorage are vulnerable to XSS
security.mitigation: Use HttpOnly cookies for token storage; implement CSRF protection
Implement refresh token rotation on every use

compliance: GDPR: Ensure user consent logged and auditable
CCPA: Support user data deletion from OAuth sessions
recommendations: Implement token caching layer (Redis) for validation
Use HttpOnly cookies for token storage
Encrypt tokens at rest in database
Create separate identity microservice for scaling

stage3_implementationPlan.overview: Implement OAuth 2.0 in 3 phases: (1) Core identity service + token generation, (2) API gateway integration + client libraries, (3) Token caching, monitoring, and load testing.

phases.name: Core Identity Service
phases.description: Build identity microservice handling OAuth 2.0 flow, token generation, validation, and database schema for tokens/consents.

tasks.title: Design OAuth 2.0 token and consent schema
tasks.description: Create PostgreSQL schema for tokens, refresh tokens, and user consents

tasks.title: Implement token generation service
tasks.description: Build JWT token generation with configurable expiration and claims

tasks.title: Implement token validation service
tasks.description: Build token verification with signature validation and expiration checks

tasks.title: Integrate with Auth0
tasks.description: Set up Auth0 tenant and OAuth provider integration

phases.name: API Gateway Integration
phases.description: Update API gateway to validate OAuth tokens, provide client libraries, and support legacy API keys alongside new OAuth.

tasks.title: Implement OAuth middleware in API gateway
tasks.description: Add middleware to validate OAuth tokens on protected endpoints

tasks.title: Create OAuth client libraries (SDK)
tasks.description: Build SDKs for web, mobile, and server-side OAuth client flows

tasks.title: Support legacy API key auth alongside OAuth
tasks.description: Implement dual authentication: OAuth tokens + existing API keys

tasks.title: Update API documentation
tasks.description: Document new OAuth endpoints, flows, and migration guide

phases.name: Optimization, Testing & Deployment
phases.description: Add caching layer, comprehensive testing, monitoring setup, and production deployment.

tasks.title: Implement Redis token caching
tasks.description: Add Redis cache for token validation to reduce database load

tasks.title: Comprehensive testing (unit, integration, load)
tasks.description: Write and run tests; perform load testing for 1000+ concurrent sessions

tasks.title: Set up monitoring and alerting
tasks.description: Configure metrics, dashboards, and alerts for OAuth service health

tasks.title: Canary deployment and production rollout
tasks.description: Deploy to staging, run smoke tests, gradual rollout to production

technicalApproach.architecture: Microservices: Dedicated OAuth identity service + API gateway middleware
deferredDecisions: Which OAuth provider (Auth0, Okta, custom): Decision pending stakeholder meeting

riskMitigation.mitigation: Use HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite cookies; implement CSP headers
Implement Redis caching; load test with 2000+ concurrent sessions
Audit token storage/deletion; ensure user consent logging

successMetrics: Zero breaking changes to existing API clients
User adoption: 80% SSO usage within 30 days
stage3_implementationPlan.rollbackPlan: Keep legacy API key authentication active. If OAuth service fails, API gateway falls back to API key validation. Rollback deployment: revert to previous image, trigger data migration to restore token state.

stage4_testPlan.overview: Comprehensive testing strategy covering OAuth flows, token lifecycle, performance, security, and compatibility.

e2eScenarios: User login → token grant → API call

testCases.title: Authorization Code Flow - Happy Path
testCases.description: User initiates OAuth flow, receives authorization code, exchanges for access token
steps: Redirected to app with authorization code
Backend exchanges code for access token
testCases.expectedResult: Access token received, user logged in, cookie set

testCases.title: Token Refresh - Expired Access Token
testCases.description: User has expired access token; refresh token used to obtain new access token
preconditions: User authenticated with valid refresh token
steps: Attempt API call with expired token
testCases.expectedResult: New valid access token issued, user can continue without re-authentication

testCases.title: Token Validation - Invalid Signature
testCases.description: Malformed or tampered token is rejected

testCases.title: Concurrent SSO Sessions
testCases.description: Multiple users can maintain concurrent OAuth sessions without interference
steps: Each user makes simultaneous API calls
testCases.expectedResult: All users authenticated; token validation latency < 200ms p95

testCases.title: Legacy API Key Fallback
testCases.description: Old clients using API keys can still authenticate after OAuth rollout
preconditions: Legacy API key auth enabled in gateway
steps: Old client makes API request with API key
API gateway checks for OAuth token (missing)
API gateway checks for API key (present)
testCases.expectedResult: Request succeeds with API key authentication

securityTests: Token theft via XSS prevention (HttpOnly cookies)
CSRF token validation on OAuth endpoints
regressionTests: All existing API endpoints work with OAuth tokens
Legacy API key auth still functional
No performance degradation compared to baseline

stage5_releaseReadinessReport.executiveSummary: OAuth 2.0 implementation is production-ready. All critical path items completed, tests pass, security review cleared with conditions addressed, documentation complete. Recommend canary deployment at 5% traffic, monitor for 48 hours before full rollout.
recommendations: Monitor token validation latency and cache hit rates
Ensure on-call support familiar with OAuth service
Prepare customer communication for SSO availability

postReleaseChecklist.item: Verify no spike in authentication errors
Collect user feedback on SSO experience
Review security logs for suspicious token patterns

approvals.comment: Code quality excellent, all tests passing. Safe to deploy.
Approved with condition: ensure HttpOnly cookie enforcement in production.
Ready for launch. Plan customer communication before rollout.

metadata.description: Golden PDLC dataset: Complete OAuth 2.0 SSO feature from requirements through release approval. Real-world feature demonstrating full product development lifecycle with all 5 stages and realistic data.
